name: Grilled Salmon Fillets in Lettuce with Mustar
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse salmon with cold water and pat dry with paper towels.
2. Combine lemon juice, white wine, olive oil, capers, and 1/4 teaspoon mustard in a bowl. Season to taste with salt. Pour the marinade over the salmon and marinate for 30 minutes, turning once. Reserve the marinade.
3. Blanch lettuce leaves in boiling water for 5 seconds. Drain well.
4. Arrange 2 lettuce leaves with stem ends overlapping at the center. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of marinade over the lettuce.
5. Place 1 salmon fillet on the lettuce. Top with 1 or 2 more teaspoons of marinade, including some capers. Roll up the fillet.
6. Place 2 lettuce leaves on top and tuck the edges under the fish. Bring up the bottom leaf edges.
7. Tie the package securely with string. Repeat with remaining lettuce, marinade, and fillets to make 4 packages in all. Set aside.
8. To make the mustard sauce, blend butter and flour in a double boiler until smooth. Add vinegar, boiling water, bouillon cube half, dry mustard, sugar, and egg. Cook and stir over hot water until the sauce thickens for about 10 minutes. Keep warm.
9. Place the salmon bundles on a well-greased grill 4 to 5 inches from hot coals and cook for 5 to 6 minutes. Turn and cook an additional 5 to 6 minutes. Remove the string and serve with mustard sauce.
